channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Strategic intelligence, delivered daily. Geopolitical briefings read aloud — listen on the go.
channel.itunes:author: Frontion News
channel.itunes:summary: Frontion News delivers concise, authoritative briefings on global geopolitics, defense, energy, finance, and technology — now available as daily audio.

item.title: Strategic Brief — July 18, 2026: Iraq Bypasses Hormuz With Syria Pipeline Deal, Pirate Seizures Spread in Gulf of Aden, Magnitude 7.3 Quake Hits Mexico
item.description:
Baghdad signs a landmark deal to route oil through Syria's Mediterranean coast, Somali pirates hijack a second tanker in three months, and a powerful earthquake strikes off southern Mexico — as the US-Iran war enters its seventh night and Ukraine reels from the sacking of its defence minister.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news

item.title: Defence & Industry Brief — July 18, 2026: Pentagon Puts $12.6B on Affordable Cruise Missiles, Anduril Drone Fighter Fires Live AIM-120, US Troops Quietly Leave Estonia
item.description:
The Pentagon's affordable-mass missile program reaches $12.6B in framework agreements, Anduril's autonomous CCA fires its first live air-to-air missile, and the quiet drawdown of US forces from the Baltics raises NATO burden-sharing questions — even as Lockheed bags $13B in contracts and the Navy's hypersonic program falls two years behind.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/defence

item.title: Energy & Power Brief — July 18, 2026: Iran Signals Houthis to Close Bab el-Mandeb, Greece Blocks EU Russian LNG Sanctions, and Senate Targets Russia Energy Buyers With 100% Tariffs
item.description:
Tehran's reported order to prepare Houthi closure of the Red Sea chokepoint would create a dual-chokepoint crisis with Hormuz already at a standstill, while EU sanctions unity fractures over LNG and the US Senate escalates economic warfare on Russia's energy customers.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/energy

item.title: Finance & Markets Brief — July 18, 2026: Oil Surges 16% on the Week as Gulf Strikes Spread, Fed Hawks Break Ranks, Trump Rattles China Truce
item.description:
Brent closes above $88 and posts its biggest weekly gain since the war began as US-Iran strikes enter a seventh night and tanker mines close Hormuz, while a second Fed official warns of rate hikes and Trump's China voter-data accusations unsettle the trade truce.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/finance

item.title: Tech Brief — July 18, 2026: China Launches 29-Nation AI Governance Body, Moonshot Drops the World's Largest Open-Weight Model, Chip Stocks Hit Bear Market
item.description:
China formally establishes WAICO with 29 founding nations to rival Western AI governance frameworks, Moonshot AI unveils the 2.8T-parameter Kimi K3 open-weight model narrowing the US performance gap, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index enters bear territory with a 20% decline from its June peak.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/tech

item.title: Strategic Brief — July 17, 2026: US Strikes Iranian Bridges as War Escalates, Ukraine Ousts Defence Minister in Political Crisis, China Purges Top Civilian Leader
item.description:
The US hits Iranian civilian infrastructure for the first time in the current campaign, Vance accuses Israel of manipulating American opinion to prolong the war, and Zelenskyy sacks his popular defence minister — triggering street protests and a leadership crisis amid Russia's invasion.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news

item.title: Defence & Industry Brief — July 17, 2026: Zelensky Sacks Drone Architect Fedorov, UK Fast-Tracks Nightfall Missile, IEA Warns China Rare Earth Curbs Threaten $6.5T
item.description:
Ukraine's defense minister behind its drone warfare program was dismissed amid public protests, the UK pared down requirements to deliver its first ballistic missile in 50 years by 2027, and the IEA quantified the staggering economic risk of China's rare earth export controls — even as a $1.96B Saudi arms sale, a $400M hypersonic boost for Kratos, and a Pentagon autonomous drone program all advanced.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/defence

item.title: Energy & Power Brief — July 17, 2026: IEA Warns $6.5T at Risk From China Rare Earth Curbs, US LNG Set to Become Second-Largest Export Industry, and Hormuz Traffic Collapses to 13 Ships
item.description:
The IEA's Global Critical Minerals Outlook puts a staggering number on China's leverage over downstream manufacturing, S&P Global projects LNG will transform the US trade balance within five years, and the Strait of Hormuz has gone from a trickle to nearly empty as US-Iran strikes enter a sixth consecutive day.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/energy

item.title: Finance & Markets Brief — July 17, 2026: Gulf Economies Face Deeper Contractions as Yen Tests 40-Year Lows, ECB Hawks Eye September Hike
item.description:
A Reuters poll shows most Gulf economies contracting harder than expected as Hormuz disruptions make it impossible to sell oil even at higher prices, while Japan's finance minister signals decisive yen intervention and ECB officials push for a September rate hike as European gas prices surge.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/finance

item.title: Tech Brief — July 17, 2026: TSMC Commits Another $100B to Arizona, DeepSeek Hits $67B Valuation, and IEA Flags $6.5T Minerals Risk
item.description:
TSMC's massive new US commitment brings its total Arizona investment to $265B; DeepSeek's implied valuation soars as it prepares an IPO; the IEA quantifies the full scale of China's rare earth leverage over global industry.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/tech

item.title: Strategic Brief — July 16, 2026: China Overtakes US in Global Favorability for First Time, Meloni Loses Electoral Reform Vote, France Legalises Assisted Dying
item.description:
A Pew Research survey finds more people in 25 of 36 countries view China favourably over the US — the first such result in two decades of tracking. Italy's governing coalition suffers a one-vote defeat on electoral reform. France's National Assembly passes an assisted-dying law after years of deadlock.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news

item.title: Defence & Industry Brief — July 16, 2026: Europe Launches Bliksem EXO Interceptor Consortium, Air Force Buys Missiles by the Thousand, Senate Blocks $1T Defense Bill
item.description:
Five European firms form a consortium to build an exo-atmospheric missile interceptor targeting Russia's Oreshnik, the US Air Force signs framework deals for 28,000 cheap cruise missiles, and Senate Democrats tank the annual defense authorization over the Iran war.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/defence

item.title: Energy & Power Brief — July 16, 2026: IRGC Threatens All Gulf Export Routes as Fujairah Goes Dark, India Doubles Diesel Export Tax, and Senate Advances Russia Sanctions Bill
item.description:
Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared all Gulf energy export routes legitimate targets after US strikes hit Greater Tunb and disabled a blockade-running tanker. Fujairah port operations have effectively ceased after IRGC missile strikes on two supertankers. India hiked diesel and jet fuel export duties nearly 80% as Brent held above $85. The Senate introduced the Sanctioning Russia Act with up to 100% tariffs on top Russian energy buyers.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/energy

item.title: Finance & Markets Brief — July 16, 2026: Senate Targets India With 100% Russia Oil Tariffs as Bank of Korea Starts Tightening, Warsh Draws the Line
item.description:
A bipartisan Senate bill proposes 100% tariffs on countries buying Russian oil, with India the most exposed target, as the Bank of Korea broke a three-and-a-half-year freeze with its first rate hike. Fed Chair Warsh used his first congressional testimony to set a hawkish tone despite June's best inflation data in years, while the US-Iran ceasefire collapsed into full military escalation.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/finance

item.title: Tech Brief — July 16, 2026: CXMT's $8.6B IPO Tests the Limits of Blacklisting, Apple Knocks on Blacklisted Doors, and White House Launches Gold Eagle
item.description:
China's DRAM champion CXMT doubles its IPO target to $8.6B while Apple lobbies to buy from Pentagon-blacklisted chipmakers — exposing the gap between export controls and supply reality. Meanwhile, the White House launches an AI-powered cybersecurity clearinghouse, and ZTE wins access to Nvidia H200 chips despite its national-security designation.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/tech

item.title: Strategic Brief — July 15, 2026: China Growth Misses Target, Senate Blocks Defence Bill Over Iran Provisions, US Consumer Prices Drop Sharply
item.description:
China's Q2 GDP slips to 4.3%, Senate Democrats sink the defence authorisation over war powers, and US inflation posts its biggest monthly drop in years — but the reprieve may not last as oil surges on Hormuz disruptions.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news

item.title: Defence & Industry Brief — July 15, 2026: Nine Nations Back Ukraine's Freyja Interceptor, US Ammo Production Stalls, NATO Picks Saab Over Boeing for AWACS Replacement
item.description:
Nine European nations formally joined Ukraine's Freyja anti-ballistic missile coalition with a one-year deployment target, a DoD Inspector General report revealed the Army's 155-mm shell production is stuck at 36% of its goal, and NATO chose Saab's GlobalEye over Boeing's E-7 Wedgetail for its next radar plane.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/defence

item.title: Energy & Power Brief — July 15, 2026: Oil Surges Past $87 on Dual-Front Escalation, China Crude Imports Hit Decade Low
item.description:
Oil prices breach $87 as Hormuz disruptions and Canadian wildfire shutdowns compound supply fears, while China's crude imports slump to a decade low.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/energy

item.title: Finance & Markets Brief — July 15, 2026: US Inflation Drops Sharply as Senate Blocks War Spending, New York Bans Data Centre Permits
item.description:
US CPI posts its biggest monthly drop in years, but the Senate blocks the $87.6B war supplemental, and New York becomes the first state to ban new data centre permits.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/finance

item.title: Tech Brief — July 15, 2026: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft, China's AI Agent Rules Take Effect, Gulf States Pivot to Sovereign Chips
item.description:
Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, China's new AI agent regulations take effect, and Gulf states push toward sovereign chip fabrication.
Read the full analysis at https://frontion.news/tech
